refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and site conditions to ensure the FR is verifiable and context-bound. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or operator control responsiveness.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle and site conditions. Validate that measured p95 ≤ CT_target. Cross-check telemetry logs for consistency. Confirm no safety alerts or operational policy violations during measurement window.
sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing and minimal variance.
FR-CT-002: The teleoperation system (if engaged) shall introduce ≤ΔT_tele latency overhead to cycle time.
FR-CT-003: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that exceed ΔT_safety budget during nominal operation.
FR-CT-004: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

candidate_dps: DP-CT-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical actuation timing and sequencing; minimize dwell times; tune operator control responsiveness.
DP-CT-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling and real-time performance feedback to operator; enable adaptive duty cycle tuning.
DP-CT-C: Allocate cycle time budgets to vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems; enforce budget compliance via design and testing.
DP-CT-D: Establish telemetry-based cycle time KPI dashboard; enable continuous monitoring and root-cause analysis of cycle time variance.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-CT-C + DP-CT-D: Allocate explicit cycle time budgets to vehicle (load/travel/dump actuation), teleoperation (latency), and safety services (interlock response time); enforce budgets via design, simulation, and testing. Implement telemetry-based cycle time monitoring and KPI dashboard to enable continuous performance tracking, variance analysis, and operator feedback.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation timing is coupled to safety interlock response times (must not block critical path).
Teleoperation latency is coupled to operator control loop stability (must remain within acceptable margin).
Telemetry reporting overhead must not degrade real-time vehicle control performance.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable via explicit budget allocation and interface contracts. DP-CT-C enforces separation of concerns (vehicle, teleoperation, safety budgets). DP-CT-D (telemetry) is decoupled from real-time control via asynchronous reporting. Recommended: define ΔT_tele and ΔT_safety as design constraints in DP refinement.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle actuation physics and site conditions (not a constraint violation).
Assumption: Teleoperation latency budget ΔT_tele is known and acceptable to operator.
Assumption: Safety interlock response time budget ΔT_safety does not conflict with cycle time target.
Risk: If vehicle actuation is fundamentally slower than CT_target, no DP can recover the gap; requires FR negotiation.
Risk: Telemetry overhead or network congestion could degrade cycle time monitoring fidelity.
Risk: Operator feedback loop (DP-CT-D) may introduce behavioral variance not captured in nominal testing.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-CT-C to explicitly define cycle time budget allocation table: Vehicle (T_vehicle), Teleoperation (T_tele), Safety (T_safety), Telemetry (T_telemetry), with T_vehicle + T_tele + T_safety + T_telemetry ≤ CT_target. Refine DP-CT-D to specify telemetry sampling rate, latency, and KPI calculation method (e.g., rolling p95 window). Add design constraint: safety interlocks must not introduce blocking delays > ΔT_safety during nominal operation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time metrics (load start, travel start, dump start, dump end timestamps; calculated p95 cycle time; variance; operator feedback) to external monitoring/dashboard systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle definition, site conditions, and CT_target from Site Manager or system configuration.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts and interlock status from Safety Services to validate that cycle time optimization does not violate safety constraints.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of cycle time KPI. It orchestrates vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems and owns the budget allocation logic and telemetry aggregation. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry reporting and safety alert ingestion cross boundaries.

Vehicle owns load/travel/dump actuation timing but does not own teleoperation latency or safety interlock budgets. Cycle time KPI is a system-level concern, not a vehicle-only concern. High diffusion: cycle time depends on teleoperation and safety services.

Teleoperation owns latency budget but does not own vehicle actuation or safety interlock timing. Cycle time KPI is a system-level concern. High diffusion: cycle time depends on vehicle and safety services.

Safety Services owns interlock response time budget but does not own vehicle actuation or teleoperation latency. Cycle time KPI is a system-level concern. High diffusion: cycle time depends on vehicle and teleoperation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) cycle time KPI is a system-level performance metric that integrates vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems; (2) NGHE Solution owns the budget allocation logic and enforcement; (3) NGHE Solution aggregates telemetry from all subsystems and reports KPI; (4) containment score is 95 with minimal diffusion (5). Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are contributors but not owners.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution does not have visibility into real-time vehicle actuation timing, teleoperation latency, or safety interlock delays, telemetry-based KPI reporting may be inaccurate or delayed.
Risk: If cycle time budget allocation is not enforced at design time (DP level), NGHE Solution may not be able to guarantee CT_target achievement.
Risk: If telemetry reporting introduces significant overhead, it could degrade cycle time performance (self-defeating).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define explicit interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services for cycle time budget reporting and telemetry ingestion. Specify telemetry sampling rate and latency to ensure KPI reporting does not degrade performance. Add design constraint: NGHE Solution shall not introduce > T_telemetry overhead to cycle time.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR clearly specifies the cycle time KPI (p95 ≤ CT_target) and measurement context (duty cycle, site conditions, telemetry). Selected DP (budget allocation + telemetry monitoring) directly addresses the FR by: (1) allocating cycle time budgets to vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems to ensure CT_target is achievable; (2) implementing telemetry-based monitoring to verify KPI achievement. DP is traceable to FR and does not introduce unnecessary complexity.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry reporting overhead must be budgeted and monitored to avoid self-defeating performance impact.
Safety interlock response time budget must be coordinated between Safety Services and NGHE Solution to avoid conflicts.
Teleoperation latency budget must be validated against operator control loop stability requirements.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for cycle time KPI ownership. It owns budget allocation logic, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are contributors that must report cycle time metrics to NGHE Solution via defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert). Containment score is high (95); diffusion is low (5).

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via telemetry (timestamps for load start, travel start, dump start, dump end). p95 calculation is straightforward statistical analysis. Verification can be performed via field testing under agreed duty cycle and site conditions. Acceptance criteria are clear and objective.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution as the top-level owner. Sub-allocations to Vehicle (load/travel/dump timing), Teleoperation (latency), and Safety Services (interlock response time) are clear and traceable. Budget allocation table (recommended in DP revision) will provide explicit allocation clarity.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-CT-C to include explicit cycle time budget allocation table with T_vehicle, T_tele, T_safety, T_telemetry budgets and sum constraint.
Refine DP-CT-D to specify telemetry sampling rate, latency, and KPI calculation method (rolling p95 window, update frequency).
Define interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services for cycle time metric reporting.
Add design constraint: safety interlocks must not introduce blocking delays > ΔT_safety during nominal operation.
Add design constraint: telemetry reporting must not introduce > T_telemetry overhead to cycle time.

decision.justification: Refined FR is clear, DP is sound and addresses the FR, and IM assignment is appropriate. However, the DP requires further refinement at the next level to define explicit cycle time budget allocation, telemetry reporting specifications, and interface contracts. Sub-FRs (FR-CT-001 through FR-CT-004) are well-defined and ready for decomposition. Proceeding to next level decomposition is justified.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing and minimal variance.
FR-CT-002: The teleoperation system (if engaged) shall introduce ≤ΔT_tele latency overhead to cycle time.
FR-CT-003: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that exceed ΔT_safety budget during nominal operation.
FR-CT-004: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle actuation physics and site conditions.
Teleoperation latency budget ΔT_tele is known and acceptable to operator.
Safety interlock response time budget ΔT_safety does not conflict with cycle time target.
Telemetry reporting overhead is negligible or budgeted separately.
Site conditions (terrain, load mass, distance) are stable and representative of duty cycle.
open_risks: If vehicle actuation is fundamentally slower than CT_target, no DP can recover the gap; requires FR negotiation with customer.
Telemetry overhead or network congestion could degrade cycle time monitoring fidelity.
Operator feedback loop (DP-CT-D) may introduce behavioral variance not captured in nominal testing.
Safety interlock response time may conflict with cycle time optimization; requires careful budget allocation.
Teleoperation latency may exceed acceptable margin under poor network conditions; requires contingency planning.
evidence_needed: Vehicle actuation timing specifications (load, travel, dump cycle times) and achievable range.
Teleoperation latency measurements under nominal and degraded network conditions.
Safety interlock response time specifications and acceptable delay budget.
Telemetry reporting overhead analysis (CPU, network bandwidth, latency impact).
Field test data: measured cycle time distribution (mean, p95, variance) under agreed duty cycle and site conditions.
Operator feedback on cycle time performance and control responsiveness.
